skill.name: nano-image-generator
skill.description: Generate image assets with Gemini 3 Pro Preview using text prompts, aspect ratios, output sizes, and optional reference images for style transfer or character consistency.
skill.summary: Generate image assets from prompts and reference images using Gemini 3 Pro Preview.
skill.author: lxfater

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, crypto, and obfuscation findings, but most markdown command examples and magic-byte checks are false positives. Confirmed risks remain: the script asks users to hardcode an API key, sends prompts and reference images to Google Gemini, and reads or writes user-specified files. No prompt injection text or confirmed malicious intent was found, so the skill is not blocked, but it should not publish until secret handling and privacy warnings are improved.

high_findings.title: API Key Stored Directly in Source Code
high_findings.description: The setup flow tells users to replace a placeholder inside scripts/generate_image.py with a real Gemini API key. Storing secrets in source files increases the chance that the key is committed, shared, or exposed in backups.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code and both README files explicitly direct users to edit the script and place the API key in source. This is a confirmed secret-management weakness, although it is not evidence of malicious intent.

high_findings.title: Prompts and Reference Images Sent to External API
high_findings.description: The script sends the user prompt and any provided reference images to the Google Gemini API. This is expected functionality, but it can disclose private images, designs, or confidential prompts to a third-party service.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The request URL, request construction, and inline reference image payload are directly visible. The behavior is intentional for image generation, but the privacy impact is real.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled File Reads and Writes
medium_findings.description: The script reads each reference image path supplied through --ref and writes the generated image to a user-supplied output path. This is normal for the tool, but callers should avoid sensitive input paths and validate output destinations in shared environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit and user controlled. The risk is moderate because the tool does not automatically search files, but it can process sensitive paths if instructed.

medium_findings.title: API Key Included in Request URL
medium_findings.description: The Gemini API key is appended to the request URL as a query parameter. Query parameters can appear in debugging output, proxy logs, or telemetry in some environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL construction with the key parameter is direct evidence. It may match Google API conventions, but it still increases exposure risk compared with secret storage outside source and careful log handling.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Many static external command findings are command examples in README and SKILL documentation. They describe how to invoke the script and are not executable code inside the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced markdown examples, not runtime shell execution. This strongly supports a false-positive assessment for that static category.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Obfuscation Alerts Appear Benign
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and hex-obfuscation alerts correspond to image format magic-byte checks or ordinary documentation text. No encryption, hashing, or hidden decoder behavior was found in the reviewed lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hex byte constants identify PNG, JPEG, WebP, and GIF headers, and base64 decoding handles API image responses. These are legitimate image-processing patterns.

low_findings.title: Hidden Claude Settings Path Mentioned in Documentation
low_findings.description: The README files mention ~/.claude/settings.json for installation. This is a documentation reference to Claude configuration, not code that reads or modifies the hidden file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are install instructions only. There is no script behavior that accesses the hidden settings file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Secret Placeholder Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The script relies on replacing a source-code placeholder with a real API key instead of reading from a secret store or environment variable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Both implementation and documentation confirm the pattern. This is a common source of accidental credential disclosure.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Request with User Content
dangerous_patterns.description: The script builds a JSON request containing user prompt text and optional image data, then posts it to an external Google API endpoint.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The payload construction and urlopen call are direct evidence. This is intended behavior, but it is a sensitive data-flow pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Files Converted to Inline API Payloads
dangerous_patterns.description: Reference images are read from local paths, base64 encoded, and embedded in the outbound API request.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file read and inlineData construction are visible in adjacent functions. This creates a clear path from local files to a network request.

content.value_statement: Teams need quick visual assets without leaving their coding workflow. This skill runs a Gemini image generation script with prompts, aspect ratios, sizes, and optional reference images.
actual_capabilities: Generates images from text prompts through the Gemini 3 Pro Preview image API.
Accepts up to 14 reference images for style transfer or character consistency.
Supports square, portrait, landscape, mobile, and cinematic aspect ratios.
Detects returned image format and writes the generated file with a matching extension.
Creates parent output directories when saving generated images.
limitations: Requires a Gemini API key before the script can run.
Current setup instructs users to place the API key directly in source code.
Prompts and reference images are sent to Google Gemini for processing.
Text inside generated images may be inaccurate and should be checked manually.

use_cases.title: Create App Store Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate app icons, splash visuals, and feature graphics with consistent aspect ratios and output sizes.

use_cases.title: Produce Campaign Graphics
use_cases.description: Create banners, social posts, and launch visuals from prompts while reusing a reference style.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Illustrations
use_cases.description: Draft visual concepts for UI states, onboarding scenes, and feature explanations during planning.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [subject] for [purpose]. Use a [style] look and save it to [output path].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Sized Asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Asset with aspect ratio and resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [visual asset type] for [project]. Use aspect ratio [ratio], size [1K/2K/4K], and save it to [output path].

prompt_templates.title: Match a Reference Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [new content] in the same visual style as [reference image path]. Keep the color palette and mood consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Consistent Image Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a series of [number] images for [campaign or feature]. Use [reference paths] for style consistency and save each file in [folder].

output_examples.input: Generate a square app icon for a task planning app with a clean geometric style.
output_examples.output: A saved square image asset with a modern task planning symbol, balanced colors, and an app icon composition.

output_examples.input: Create a 16:9 hero banner for a productivity launch using a calm blue and white visual direction.
output_examples.output: A landscape banner image suitable for a website hero area, saved with the detected image format extension.

output_examples.input: Use a character reference image and place the same character in a warm cafe scene.
output_examples.output: A generated scene that attempts to preserve the reference character while changing the setting and lighting.

best_practices: Use environment-based secret handling instead of placing API keys in source files.
Avoid sending confidential prompts, unreleased designs, or private reference images to external APIs.
Specify purpose, composition, style, aspect ratio, and output path in each request.
anti_patterns: Do not commit a modified script that contains a real Gemini API key.
Do not use sensitive personal images as references without consent and policy review.
Do not rely on generated text inside images for final production copy.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The script requires a Gemini API key before image generation can run.

faq.question: Can it use reference images?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports repeated reference image arguments and limits requests to 14 reference images.

faq.question: Where are generated images saved?
faq.answer: Images are saved to the output path supplied by the user. Parent directories are created when needed.

faq.question: Does it send data to an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. Prompts and reference images are sent to the Google Gemini API for generation.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish in its current form?
faq.answer: No. The current API key setup stores secrets in source code and needs remediation before publication.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
